root.speaker: Fiddler Marn
root.text:
Eh! Come sit. You ever learn a tune you couldn't read?
Best way. Hands learn faster than eyes.

uncle.speaker: Fiddler Marn
uncle.text:
Uncles. Three of 'em. Couldn't read a word between 'em
but they could pull a wedding out of a Tuesday.

play.speaker: Fiddler Marn
play.text:
*Marn draws the bow. A short, bright tune that sounds
like running water and stamping feet at once. Then a
long note that fades just as you notice it's there.*

props.message:
Fiddle-on-the-fence —

taught by uncles who could not read.

A fiddle leans against the fence-post, bow tucked under
the strings. Anyone who passes can pick it up. Most do.
